AIOps - HealthCheck Runner
__MAIN_MENU__

HealthCheck User Guide

__DOC_VERSION__ | __DOC_RULE__
Back to Docs

Outline

1. Startup and Access 2. Home Page Inspection Setup 3. Job Status Page 4. AI Analysis Workflow 5. Analysis Execution Options 6. Prompt and Template Management 7. Deployment Dependencies 8. FAQ

1. Startup and Access

Use `./run.sh` (recommended: auto venv/deps setup, port cleanup, hot reload). Default URL is `http://127.0.0.1:8080`.

cd healthcheck ./run.sh # Compatibility entries ./scripts/start_web.sh python3 web_runner.py python3 app/web_server.py

2. Home Page Inspection Setup

  • Input device SSH username/password.
  • Select jump mode if needed: direct / Jump SSH / SMC command mode.
  • Fill device addresses (one per line), or import a device file.
  • Select check items and optional custom commands (executed line by line).
  • Save current selected checks + custom commands as check template for reuse.
  • Set execution mode, workers, retry count, and debug switch.
  • Click "Run Python Healthcheck".

Tip: Home page supports local persistence. Use "Clear Saved Config" to reset.

3. Job Status Page

  • Displays live logs and status badges (running/completed/failed).
  • After success, current JSON/CSV reports are available for download.
  • You can return to home page for the next inspection.

4. AI Analysis Workflow

  • When clicking "AI Analysis": historical report is preferred if uploaded.
  • If no historical report is uploaded, current task report is analyzed.
  • If neither exists, the page prompts to run inspection or import report first.
  • Use "Analysis Precheck" to view estimated calls/tokens/time before execution.
  • Key buttons in AI Settings: `Import API Key`, `Test Connection`, `Save Config`.
  • Normal mode: key evidence + controlled truncation to avoid context overflow.
  • Batch mode: analyze per device, then aggregate.
  • Chunk mode: split each device by check groups, then aggregate.
  • Cloud model calls use unified strategy: model discovery + candidate matching + endpoint fallback.
  • Shows current token and cumulative token after analysis.
  • "Save Analysis Report" exports markdown (.md), with Save-As dialog fallback to download.

5. Analysis Execution Options

  • Batch mode: per-device analysis; disabled means single overall analysis request.
  • Chunk mode: only effective for structured JSON, splitting by check items.
  • AI concurrency (device level): max devices analyzed in parallel.
  • Chunks per device: only effective in chunk mode.
  • Retries per device: retries only failed AI calls, not data collection.

6. Prompt and Template Management

  • System prompts define strict output behavior and format.
  • Task prompts define scenario-specific analysis goals.
  • You can import prompts into System Prompt or Task Prompt.
  • Review/edit/save/delete are supported with confirmation dialogs.
  • Check templates can store selected checks + custom commands.

7. Deployment Dependencies

  • Python 3.9+.
  • Dependencies: `pip install paramiko PyYAML certifi`.
  • Ensure `config/command_map.yaml` exists (or upload via UI).
  • Ensure writable dirs: `output/reports`, `runtime/tmp`, `state`, `prompts/*`.
  • Ensure network access to device SSH (22) and selected model API domains.
  • For local LLM mode, ensure LM Studio URL is reachable and model is loaded.
  • For SSL issues with cloud providers, use `scripts/start_web.sh`.
cd healthcheck python3 -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate pip install -U pip pip install paramiko PyYAML certifi python -m pip install -r requirements.txt ./scripts/start_web.sh

8. FAQ

  • Page keeps spinning: enable debug mode to inspect full runtime logs.
  • Model connection test failed: verify model endpoint, API key, and network path.
  • If you see `Address already in use`, start with `./run.sh` to auto-clean old port occupiers.
  • No feedback after saving model config: new version shows confirmation prompt and success alert.
  • No native Save-As popup: browser may not support File System Access API; download fallback is used.
  • Icons not shown: system falls back to built-in icon and analysis is not affected.
  • If report is too large and analysis fails, enable batch mode and optionally chunk mode.
